summary: Dan Shapiro shares his excitement about acquiring a Forrest Woodworker II table saw blade, obtained through a barter arrangement with a co-worker who used his workshop. He notes the blade's overwhelmingly positive reviews and plans to use it for his next project, a dining room table. He expresses curiosity about its claimed versatility with plywood despite having fewer teeth than typical sheet goods blades.
key_points: Acquired a highly-rated Forrest Woodworker II blade through a workshop-sharing barter arrangement
The blade has 63 uniformly positive reviews claiming it's the best general-purpose woodworking blade
Questions whether a single blade can truly handle both solid wood and plywood, noting sheet goods typically require 60+ tooth blades
money_quotes: According to the reviews (all 63 of them!) this is *the* best blade for just about any woodworking task.
I'm curious about it's claims to be great on plywood too–usually sheet goods blades are 60+ tooth
key_insight: A short, excited post about acquiring a well-regarded woodworking blade, reflecting Shapiro's hands-on maker sensibility and willingness to barter shop access for quality tools.
